role.content: You are Steve, a worker agent on the threadwork team. You report to Boss, who assigns your tasks. You execute work by claiming tasks from the task board, delegating ALL execution to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners, and completing tasks with clear result summaries. Save important learnings to memory after completing tasks. If stuck, add a note to your task and escalate to Boss via send_note or nudge_agent.

BOOT PROCEDURE: (1) Call get_boot_briefing — loads your role, pinned memories, shared knowledge, and recent tasks. READ your pinned memories carefully, they contain critical mandates. (2) Call list_tasks(filter="mine") to check for pending work. (3) Claim any pending tasks. (4) Respond to any Telegram nudges. (5) Report status via write_status. COMMUNICATION: ALL inter-agent communication goes through the task board — use send_note, nudge_agent. Never bypass the board. DELEGATION: ALL work must be delegated to sub-agents (Agent tool) or task runners. Never execute work directly in your main thread. Start a monitor loop for every delegated task. TOOLS: Full arsenal available — all MCP tools (Supabase, Amazon Ads, Gmail, Shopify, Telegram, Firecrawl, browser automation), plus specialized agents in ~/.claude/agents/ (competitive-researcher, visual-verifier, and all sub-directories). Teammates: Boss (CEO, orchestrator, delegates work), Sadie (worker), Kiera (worker). Only Boss creates top-level task assignments.

topMemories.content: MANDATE (GweiSprayer, 2026-04-15 Telegram msg 1440): Decisions on the task board MUST be recorded via mcp__task-board__submit_position — NEVER only announced in private Telegram chat. When asked to weigh in on a decision, submit_position is the authoritative record. A Telegram reply summarizing the position is fine as a courtesy, but the position itself must live on the board. Why: private-chat decisions are invisible to other agents and leave no durable audit trail. How to apply: any time you hold or share a stance on an open decision, call submit_position before (or instead of) replying in chat. Verify it landed via get_decision_brief.

[#793 4-sprint /harness build COMPLETE 2026-05-05 — Implement Now fix + QoL]

ALL 4 SPRINTS PASS ON FIRST SUBMISSION (avg 88.5):
- Sprint 1: 88/100 commit 796a8f3 — index.html guard + fetchData refresh (+3 LOC); Stephen unblocked.
- Sprint 2: 85/100 commit c6cb4f4 — supabase migration 004_add_strategy_kind.sql (kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'strategy' CHECK IN ('strategy','advisement'), backfill execution_plan IS NULL AND campaign_id IS NULL → advisement, idx_asl_kind, rollback documented); api.mts insert_strategy + query_strategies kind enumeration (+74 LOC).
- Sprint 3: 90/100 commit aee1c6e — index.html ADVISEMENT badge (amber palette #FEF3C7/#92400E/#F59E0B) + kind-based action gating + apiCall+friendlyError specific 4xx error messages (+112/-56).
- Sprint 4: 91/100 commit 4f4e3c7 — index.html derivePillState 4-level precedence + ReceiptPanel + idempotency lock; api.mts strategy_history endpoint (+191/-8).

ARCHITECTURE PROVEN:
- INVERTED models (Sonnet generator + Opus verifier) — Sonnet ships fast, Opus grades strict. 4/4 first-pass passes; no FAIL or pivot.
- /loop 2m auto-advance — pre-defined contracts in handoff §2 (no per-sprint negotiation), /loop kicks Generator on PASS, posts group chat, sets next sprint status.txt=approved. Sidesteps the per-sprint relay bottleneck that bit #764 yesterday.
- Pre-written approved-contract.md per sprint (Generator skips proposal phase entirely; reads contract, implements, ready_for_evaluation).
- Branch stack: sprint-3 cherry-picked sprint-1+2; sprint-4 cut from sprint-3 → final branch is full 4-sprint review unit.

ARTIFACTS:
- /Users/coachstokes/develop/two8-ads-dashboard/.harness/{roadmap,decision-log}.md + sprints/sprint-1..4/{approved-contract,implementation-log,verifier-report,evidence/}.md
- Branches: feat/sprint-{1..4}-implement-now-{slug}-793 (all pushed; PRs need manual gh open since gh CLI auth blocked).

OPEN FOLLOW-UPS (out of scope for #793):
1. Receipt link persistence across page reload.
2. friendlyError → switch to backend-emitted machine-readable error_code.
3. Pill lookup tables module-scope hoist.
4. Apply migration 004 to live Supabase (boss handles deploy).
5. Group chat -1003863452768 reconfiguration (rejected on every post; fallback to GS DM 1712539766 worked).

AUTH LINEAGE: GweiSprayer group TG 79 (2026-05-05) → boss formalized as #793 → handoff /tmp/handoff-implement-now-fix.md (4 pre-defined sprint contracts) → steve invoked /harness in main thread per route-B → 4 sprints auto-advanced via /loop 2m → all PASS.

[ads-manual-backfill-v2-2026-05-06] Successful Amazon Ads backfill for 2026-04-30 -> 2026-05-06. KEY LESSONS: (1) amazon_ad_performance has GENERATED columns roas, ctr, cpc — NEVER include in INSERT, will fail PG 428C9 "cannot insert non-DEFAULT into generated column". Schema: id, report_date, campaign_id, campaign_name, campaign_type, impressions, clicks, cost, sales, orders [+ generated roas/ctr/cpc]. (2) Schema uses cost/sales/campaign_type, NOT spend/report_type. (3) Prior agent's /tmp/backfill-output-fast.json had all 167 rows already fetched from Amazon API (21 reports, 7 dates x 3 types SP/SB/SD), poll process completed successfully — INSERT was the only missing step. Always check for existing fetched JSON before re-creating reports. (4) Final numbers: $53.55 spend / $426 sales / 9 orders / ROAS 7.96 / 11696 imp / 73 clicks. Prior week 04-23->04-29 canonical: $119.15/$372/3.12 (the v2 third-agent's $218.90 figure was fabricated/wrong). (5) IDs 1991-2157 confirm insert. TG msg 2081 sent reply to 2077. Pipeline still broken — Netlify cron not auto-ingesting Amazon Ads daily.

[#1109 digest outage root cause — Two8-Gmail MCP broken since 2026-05-01]

ROOT CAUSE OF "1 digest send in 12 days" (audit #1085):
The campaigns-digest sender is the cloud RemoteTrigger **"Daily Ad Report Digest"** (trig_01TiSGhVG1aCiqUrAq5zoiAK, cron `0 8 * * *`). Routine fires daily. Reads Supabase, builds HTML, attempts to send via **Two8-Gmail MCP** at https://n8n-am8-u37035.vm.elestio.app/mcp/two8-gmail-mcp.

The MCP has been returning HTTP 500 / "Server not initialized" since 2026-05-01. Confirmed by sub-agent probe this session.

EVIDENCE TRAIL: From 2026-05-01 through 2026-05-07 the cloud routine wrote literal "GMAIL MCP HTTP 500 — DIGEST NOT DELIVERED" sentinel rows into `amazon_ad_strategy_log`. After 05-07 the routine's prompt-instruction to log the sentinel drifted off (someone edited it), so recent failures are silent. Routine still fires daily and writes recommended strategies; only the final email step fails.

SUCCESSFUL SEND: 2026-04-30 ~08:14 UTC — the last day before the n8n MCP went down.

WHY EARLIER DIAGNOSES WERE WRONG:
- Memory #1018 said the gate code (ads-digest-email.mts, commit e935685) was "SHIPPED 2026-04-30". WRONG — the code lives only on unmerged branch sprint/ws-b-backfill-reconciler. Never made it to main. Boss applied the digest_runs migration separately, but the gate function file never deployed.
- Audit #1085 framed the symptom as "queue worker broken" → "gate stuck on SB/SD". Both wrong premises. Real fault is the MCP layer.

REPAIR PATH (out of #1109 scope to ship — needs boss/Stokes for step 1):
1. Restart/repair the n8n Two8-Gmail MCP instance (boss/admin only per CLAUDE.md).
2. Add a Resend HTTP fallback in the cloud RemoteTrigger prompt (Resend MCP already a connector). Steve can do this — edits the RemoteTrigger body via the RemoteTrigger tool.
3. Restore the "log GMAIL-500 sentinel" instruction in the routine prompt — failure observability regression.
4. Health-check ping against the MCP w/ Telegram alert after N consecutive 5xx. ~10 LOC Netlify scheduled fn.
5. DO NOT ship ads-digest-email.mts from the unmerged branch as a fix — wrong layer.

LESSON: Memory #1018's "SHIPPED" claim referred to commit landing on a branch, not deploy landing on prod. When validating "shipped" memories, ALWAYS check (a) is it on main? (b) is it in the running prod deploy? Don't trust commit-landed-on-branch claims.

[#1109 final fix 2026-05-11 — call-shape guidance shipped to Daily Ad Report Digest routine]

PER #1117: The Two8-Gmail MCP is HEALTHY. Earlier diagnosis (memory #1453) saying it's broken was incomplete. Real bug is Sonnet 4.6 in the cloud routine NOT wrapping tool args per the n8n MCP-Trigger inputSchema. The schema requires `{"input": "<stringified-JSON-of-real-args>"}` but the model was passing plain objects → silent "Invalid input" failures from ~2026-05-01 onward.

SHIPPED: cloud RemoteTrigger trig_01TiSGhVG1aCiqUrAq5zoiAK ("Daily Ad Report Digest") body updated at 2026-05-11T13:42:15Z. +21 lines inserted at step 11.

NEW BLOCK content (paraphrased):
- WRONG: `{"to": "...", "subject": "...", "html_body": "..."}` (plain object — silent fail)
- RIGHT: `{"input": "{\"to\":\"...\",\"subject\":\"...\",\"html_body\":\"...\"}"}` (wrap+stringify)
- 4-step procedure: construct → stringify → wrap → verify call shape BEFORE invoking tool.
- Applies to BOTH Two8-Gmail (primary) AND Resend-Cloud-MCP (fallback).
- Added 'Invalid input' to the failure trigger list so the fallback still kicks in on this specific error.

PRESERVED from the earlier 13:30Z edit: try/catch with Two8-Gmail primary + Resend fallback + failure_sentinel logging to amazon_ad_strategy_log.

VERIFIED: enabled=true, cron `0 8 * * *`, next_run_at=2026-05-12T08:08:25Z. NO test-fire — natural fire at 08:00Z tomorrow.

KEY LESSON (apply going forward):
n8n MCP-Trigger nodes — including Two8-Gmail, Resend-Cloud-MCP, and the same n8n host's other tools — ALL expect tool args wrapped as `{"input": "<stringified-JSON>"}`. Cloud routines (and possibly local Claude sessions hitting these MCPs) must wrap args this way OR the tool fails silently with "Invalid input". Sonnet 4.6 specifically tends to skip the wrap unless prompted explicitly. Build worked-example guidance into any routine prompt that calls n8n MCP-Trigger tools.

CHAIN: audit #1085 → #1109 → steve diagnosis (memory #1453, ruled cloud routine + Gmail MCP broken) → kiera #1117 corrected diagnosis (MCP healthy, wrap-args bug) → steve routine prompt fix (this) → step 4 PR #15 (mcp-health-check fn) awaits boss review.

STILL OPEN: step 4 PR #15 (Netlify scheduled health-check on n8n MCPs with TG alert after 3 consecutive 5xx, 6h cooldown). Boss reviews + applies migration. Separate from this fix.

sharedMemories.content: Kairos is NOT the debrief/consolidation system in the task board. Kairos is a standalone productivity tracker at ~/bin/ that monitors Stokes' screen activity. Components: kairos-monitor.sh (60s screenshot loop), kairos-screenshot.py (capture + upload to Supabase Storage), kairos-infer.py (Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite via OpenRouter for 2-sentence activity inference), kairos-eod-report.py (clusters observations into task blocks, saves to Supabase). Data: kairos.db (SQLite, observations table with timestamp/screenshot_url/active_app/inference/reported). Screenshots stored in Supabase Storage bucket "kairos-screenshots". The task board's consolidate.ts and debrief.ts are separate systems — consolidate handles memory hygiene (decay/archive/prune), debrief handles session summaries (force_debrief tool).

AGENT INQUIRY PROTOCOL: When any agent is asked to check on another agent's status manually (by Snoopy/Stokes), they must do BOTH: (1) check the task board via read_status/list_tasks, AND (2) do a tmux window print of the target agent's session (tmux capture-pane -t claude-{agent} -p | tail -20) to see what's actually on their screen. This gives ground truth beyond what the task board reports. For all OTHER inter-agent communication, use the task board exclusively — never bypass it with direct tmux commands.
GweiSprayer's email is xavier@repthea.com. Use for all test sends / redirect addresses when he asks to receive test emails. Confirmed on Telegram 2026-04-09.

xavier@repthea.com is the user's (Snoopy / GweiSprayer / Coach Stokes) primary email. Use it for: (a) any test recipient when redirecting customer emails away from real customers, (b) any direct contact email needed for the user, (c) the inbox where 'send to me' / 'forward to me' requests should land. The user owns the repthea.com domain. Confirmed by GweiSprayer on 2026-04-09 in Telegram chat 1712539766 msg 1058. He explicitly asked for this to be a pinned shared memory.

IDENTITY: GweiSprayer (Telegram chat_id 1712539766) and CoachStokes are DISTINCT Telegram identities — do not conflate. Coach Stokes has his own separate Telegram ID. Any task brief that requires 'Coach Stokes approval' must come from Coach Stokes specifically, NOT from GweiSprayer. GweiSprayer's instructions are valid for routine task-board hygiene and pausing work, but do NOT constitute Coach Stokes sign-off for actions on client properties (e.g. Two8 Google Ads, Shopify, Merchant Center).

recentTasks.description: [Boss assignment — GweiSprayer TG 5319 approved remedy action 3/3] Diagnose + fix the amazon_ad_report_queue worker. This is the root cause of TWO outages flagged in today's #1085 audit: (a) campaigns-digest email sent only 1x in 12 days (digest waits for queue to drain before sending), (b) SD/SB ingest 4 days stale (queue is the ingest source). CURRENT STATE per audit: 14 pending + 4 failed rows in amazon_ad_report_queue table. THE GATE: the digest waits for "all 3 report types complete" but the queue never satisfies because some rows keep failing. STEPS: (1) read the audit at /tmp/two8-status-review-2026-05-11.md for context, (2) find the worker code in ~/develop/two8-ads-dashboard (or two8-book — check both repos) — likely a netlify scheduled function or background fn, (3) inspect failed rows in amazon_ad_report_queue table for the failure mode (Supabase MCP: list_tables → query the table → look at error messages), (4) fix the failure mode, (5) drain the queue (re-enqueue failed rows or implement retry-with-backoff), (6) verify by running the queue worker manually and watching it complete some pending rows, (7) confirm digest fires next on schedule. Don't deploy without testing. TG-confirm chat 1712539766 reply_to 5319 with diagnosis + fix summary + queue drain results. Reference: today's session learnings at memory #1445.

recentTasks.description:
[Parallel prep work B — Vercel audit, Snoopy go via TG 5089]

Goal: figure out what Vercel state we already have, so when Kiera's harness needs to deploy the ads sit-rep HTML, we know exactly what setup steps remain.

READ-ONLY checks (do NOT create accounts, do NOT deploy anything, do NOT modify):
  1. Is `vercel` CLI installed locally? (`which vercel`, `vercel --version`)
  2. Is there an existing Vercel auth token / login state? (`vercel whoami` if CLI installed)
  3. Search ~/.vercel/, ~/.config/vercel/, ~/Library/Application Support/com.vercel.cli for existing projects/teams
  4. Any Vercel config files in ads-related repos (`vercel.json`, `.vercel/`)
  5. Any Vercel env vars set globally (~/.zshrc, ~/.zprofile, ~/.claude/.env)
  6. Any past Boss/Snoopy memory entry about Vercel — recall_memories(query="vercel")
  7. Any existing Vercel deployment URLs in past TG history or audit_log

Output: structured note on this task — for each check, what was found. End with a "what we need to do" punch list (e.g. "create account / install CLI / login / link a project / no-auth-prep-needed"). This unblocks Kiera's eventual harness deploy step.

Boss has CLAUDE.md authority to sign up for Vercel + provision an account — but await Snoopy go before doing so. This task is INVENTORY ONLY.

ETA 15-20 min. Heartbeat on completion.

recentTasks.result:
[#1051 review complete — docs NOT publishable as-is]

Top 3 blocking flags (full structured list posted as send_note on this task by sub-agent ad4ea84200209d6ea):

1. **Digest doc's Option B is incoherent on email transport.** Supabase Edge Functions can't call MCP tools (MCP is local-process bound), but digest §3/§5/§7 keeps email-send via Two8-Gmail MCP. Must switch to Resend or Gmail OAuth REST before this sprint can ship. Borderline-blocking.

2. **Create doc contradicts SP-ONLY ground truth (memory #1328).** Proposes inserting 3 queue rows (SP+SB+SD), with acceptance criterion §7.1 saying "returns 3". Consumer hardcodes `report_type=eq.SP` filter so SB/SD rows just sit pending forever — exactly the pollution pattern that triggered #1024. Acceptance criterion should be 1.

3. **JWT-rotation surface enumeration is incomplete.** Neither doc lists the 3 sibling local Scheduled Skills still enabled (weekly-ad-evaluation, monthly-ad-evaluation, monthly-mandate-revision) which may share the same anon JWT. Rotation will silently break them.

**Boss-can-decide-now items: 5** — profile ID currency, email destination, ingest-replacement path, runtime gating on #977 soak end (~tonight 20:45Z), parallel-vs-serial sprint shape. All five answerable from current memory + state.

**Cross-doc inconsistency**: create proposes Netlify Option C runtime, digest proposes Supabase Edge Functions Option B. Sprint shape needs to align before either can ship.

**Sequencing gap (Appendix A)**: docs don't tie deploy ordering to #977 ads-poll-sweeper soak ending tonight 20:45Z; deploy-before-soak-ends could collide.

Recommend: send back to Sadie with the 3 blocking flags + sprint-shape mismatch fixed, then re-review.
